Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Mar-11-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Alice Wong of McDERMOTT WILL & EMERY (310) 277-4110, appearing through CourtCall for defendant Honeywell International Inc.
Natasha V. Sumner of DENTONS US LLP (415-267-4130), appearing through CourtCall for defendant Morse TEC.
Jennifer C. Rasmussen of DEMLER, ARMSTRONG & ROWLAND, LLP (415-949-1900), appearing through CourtCall for defendant Familian Corp.
Ingrid Campagne of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendants William & Burrows, Inc. and Sloan Valve Company.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The trial date is June 28, 2021 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is July 9, 2021. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is June 24, 2021. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is June 11, 2021. Expert discovery cut-off date is June 25, 2021. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than April 12, 2021. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

No later than March 25, 2021, plaintiff must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims as to each defendant. No later than March 25, 2021, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on May 12, 2021 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than May 14, 2021. Responses to the demand are due no later than May 28, 2021. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by June 4, 2021 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, June 24, 2021 at 9 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
